steps.label: cause
steps.text: The grower measures soluble solids in grape juice, commonly expressed as Brix or an equivalent scale.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Soluble solids largely reflect sugar concentration and therefore indicate potential alcohol and one part of grape maturity.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The measurement helps inform harvest timing when combined with acidity, flavour, phenolic maturity and weather risk.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Brix informs harvest because grape sugar predicts potential alcohol, but it is not a complete maturity measure.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
